message:
fix(core): add TransactionBlockAdapter for MVCC-safe writes and fix directory entry handling

Introduce TransactionBlockAdapter that stages all block writes into
an active MVCC transaction, enabling high-level metadata helpers
(like ffs_inode::write_inode) to preserve transactional atomicity
and snapshot isolation. The adapter checks staged writes on reads
before falling back to the base device.

Fix ext4_add_dir_entry to properly handle directory block scanning:
- Skip unwritten extents instead of attempting to read them
- Distinguish NoSpace (block full, try next) from other errors
- Move parent link_count increment into ext4_add_dir_entry so it
  only happens for Dir file types, fixing incorrect link counts
  for non-directory entries
- Use parent_inode clone instead of re-reading to avoid TOCTOU

Thread RequestScope through ext4_symlink so slow-path symlinks
(those too large for inline storage) write data through the
transaction-aware ext4_write path.

Add u32::try_from bounds check for logical block calculation in
ext4_write to prevent silent truncation on very large files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
